Polarion Renewables SE — Cross-Border Transfer and Retention Workflow Note (EU MAR, GDPR, CSRD, SFDR)
Polarion Renewables SE is an EU issuer admitted to trading on the Nordhaven Growth Market (NGM‑FX) under the oversight of the Continental Securities Authority; this document outlines processing and transfer controls only.
We process limited personal data of staff, contractors, and professional contacts as controller for compliance and reporting; this note contains no unpublished financials and no deal information.
For CSRD/SFDR metrics we may process special categories (e.g., health and trade‑union affiliation) in aggregated form with safeguards under GDPR Art 9; no decisions are taken about individuals based solely on such data.
In one audit sample for control testing, a sustainability analyst (born 1986‑07, Central Port District) in the Hydro Storage Unit with corporate level L2, device tag NX‑7Q‑101, door badge A‑44‑903, and masked payroll account EU‑XX11‑004200 was logged to evidence role‑based access.
Generic HR records may include department (e.g., Finance), region (e.g., North Zone), or start month (e.g., 2024‑05) without names or direct contact details; such standalone items are not used to identify a person.
Pseudonymisation is applied to monitoring logs; token keys are held by the Data Protection Office and are accessed only for legally required investigations with approval.
Certain support logs are processed by Lyridia Cloud AG (processor) in Lyridia (a third country) under the EU Standard Contractual Clauses, with a Transfer Impact Assessment and technical measures (encryption at rest and in transit).
Remote access by the processor is restricted to ticket IDs, role titles, and timestamps; fields such as national identifiers and full account numbers are excluded by design.
MAR workflows: insider lists contain role title, organisational unit, and timestamped inclusion/removal; no trade prices or orders are stored here; retention is five years from the last update.
Market‑sounding records are maintained with consent flags and channel metadata; where MAR exemptions apply, extracts are limited and subject to legal review before disclosure.
Data subjects may submit access or objection requests via the portal at privacy.polarionrenewables.eu or the role mailbox privacy@polarionrenewables.eu; we will not request a copy of any government ID unless strictly necessary and proportionate.
Retention: device telemetry 180 days; insider‑list metadata five years; CSRD/SFDR raw inputs 12 months then aggregated; processor backups follow a 30‑day rolling purge.
Sample forms and placeholder codes may appear in screenshots (Instrument=XX‑NGM‑TEST01, Account=EU‑0000‑XXXX, Device=DEV‑0000); these are not real identifiers and are used only for training and quality checks.
